description: Magnetite is a mixed‑valence iron oxide (Fe²⁺/Fe³⁺) that crystallizes in an inverse cubic spinel structure. In stoichiometric magnetite, Fe³⁺ occupies the tetrahedral (Tet) sites, while the octahedral (Oct) sublattice is shared equally by Fe²⁺ and Fe³⁺. Magnetite occurs widely in nature as an accessory mineral in igneous, metamorphic, and sedimentary rocks, and is also produced by biological processes such as magnetotactic bacteria. Owing to its ferrimagnetic properties and electronic conductivity, magnetite is used in applications including magnetic recording, environmental remediation, catalysis, and as a functional material in geophysics and biomedical technologies.

Hematite is a ferric iron oxide (Fe₂O₃) that crystallizes in a hexagonal system. It is one of the most important iron ores and occurs in various geological settings, including sedimentary, metamorphic, and igneous rocks. Hemitite is also found in hydrothermal veins and as a secondary mineral in weathered environments.
